title: symphyotrichum puniceum f. candidum
intro: Symphyotrichum puniceum f. candidum, also known as the white cupped joe-pye weed, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, Symphyotrichum puniceum f. candidum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically as a salve or oil to aid in wound healing.
